title: Troop
description: Static definition for a troop type. See game-design/05-troop-stat-schema.md and game-design/08-troop-roster.md for the design rationale behind each field. Live per-unit state (currentHP, position, buffs) is NOT part of this schema — see TroopInstance in game-design/07-data-architecture.md.

domain.description: Which terrain/movement ruleset applies by default before per-unit overrides. "Land" means land vehicles specifically — Infantry is its own domain because Forest blocks Land vehicles but not Infantry, and Hills slow Infantry but not vehicles (see game-design/01-map-and-terrain.md). Domain also doubles as a matchable key in damageDealtModifiers/damageReceivedModifiers, same as tags.

tags.description: Category/trait tags, e.g. ["Vehicle", "Tank", "Heavy"]. Referenced by other units' damage modifier dictionaries.

hp.description: Damage the unit can take before dying.

armor.description: Flat damage reduction applied to each incoming hit, after damageReceivedModifiers are resolved, floored so a hit always does at least 1 damage. E.g. armor 5 turns a 10-damage hit into 5. Distinct from damageReceivedModifiers (which is a multiplier, not a flat subtraction). Bypassed entirely by an attacker whose damageTypes include Piercing.

damage.description: Base damage per attack, before modifiers.

range.description: Max engagement distance. Separate from visionRange. Also doubles as a line-attack's total beam length when lineAttack is true (see below) — a beam's max reach and a unit's max engagement distance are the same physical property for that weapon shape.

minRange.description: Minimum engagement distance — a dead zone this unit can't fire into, the mirror of range's max. 0 (default) means no dead zone. E.g. Earthshaker (an indirect-fire siege piece) can't hit anything adjacent to it.

splashRadius.description: 0 = single-target. 1-indexed against the impact hex from there: 1 = every other enemy on the impact hex itself (no spread to neighbors), 2 = impact hex + the ring at distance 1, 3 = + the ring at distance 2, etc. (CombatResolver._apply_attack checks `distance <= splashRadius - 1`). Mutually exclusive with lineAttack in practice (a unit picks one AoE shape or the other) though nothing enforces this in code.

lineAttack.description: If true, this attack fires a straight, 1-hex-wide beam from the attacker's own hex through the target's hex and onward to `range` hexes total, instead of splashRadius's circular AoE around a single impact point. Damages every enemy squad the beam crosses (goes through troops); the first hex carrying ANY building along the beam (friend or foe — a physical obstruction) stops it there — an enemy building on that hex takes the hit same as any other victim, a friendly one just blocks silently. A Wall never blocks a beam (edge-based, no single hex of its own). E.g. Tank Obliterator's rail gun.

visionRange.description: How far the unit can see. Kept separate from range.

speed.description: Base movement rate, in hexes per SECOND (see game-design/01-map-and-terrain.md's Movement & Positioning section). Per-tick advancement is (speed / terrainCostMultiplier) * tickDuration — terrain cost divides effective speed down, it does not multiply it up.

maxSquadSize.description: Max troops of this type that can share one SquadInstance (see game-design/07-data-architecture.md). A freshly produced troop auto-joins the nearest same-type squad below this cap, or forms a new squad of 1. Set to 1 for troops that never merge into squads (Engineer, Commander, Disruptor).

maxSquadsLed.description: Only relevant for Commander-tagged troops: how many squads this Commander can lead as a regiment (baseline 4 — see game-design/04-combat.md).

commanderTier.description: Only relevant for Commander-tagged troops: which Command Centre level unlocks this Commander (common at level 1, rare at level 2, epic at level 3 — all lower tiers stay unlocked at higher levels). See game-design/02-bases-and-buildings.md's Command Centre & the Commander Cap section and data/buildings/schema.json's commanderProgression.

terrainOverrides.description: Per-unit exceptions layered on top of the Domain's default terrain rules.

canTarget.description: Domains/tags this unit is allowed to engage, independent of range. Empty array = cannot attack at all (e.g. Engineer, Glider, Disruptor). Reserved value "Structure" covers buildings AND walls EXCEPT Defensive-category buildings (there is no separate "Building" vs "Wall" target kind, but Defensive is split out as its own reserved value — see below) — most combat troops include it by default since sieging structures is core to the conquest win condition; Sniper is a deliberate exception that omits it (see game-design/08-troop-roster.md). A "Structure" target IS auto-targeted by default, the same as an enemy troop or Defensive building, whenever no enemy troop is in range (see game-design/04-combat.md). Reserved value "Defensive" covers Defensive-category buildings specifically (Turret, Missile Launcher, Grenade Turret, Flame/Cold Turret, River Battery, Tower — see data/buildings/schema.json's category field) and must be listed separately from "Structure" to be attackable. A "Defensive" target is always auto-targeted the same as an enemy troop, since Defensive buildings actively fight back.

damageDealtModifiers.description: {tagOrDomain: multiplier} — bonus/penalty damage dealt vs. targets matching that tag or Domain value (or the reserved values "Structure" / "Defensive" — see canTarget above for the split between them). E.g. Grenadier -> {"Land": 1.5} applies only to Land-domain (vehicle) targets, not Infantry, Air, or Naval. Basekiller -> {"Defensive": 2.5} applies only to Defensive-category buildings, not other Structure targets. Missing key defaults to 1.0.

damageReceivedModifiers.description: {tagOrDomain: multiplier} — bonus/penalty damage received from attackers matching that tag, Domain value, or damageType (see damageTypes below). Missing key defaults to 1.0.

damageTypes.description: Labels describing the nature of this unit's attack, matched against targets' damageReceivedModifiers exactly like a tag/Domain (e.g. "Fire" lets Flamethrower trigger a Wood Wall's {"Fire": 2.0} entry). Kept separate from descriptive Category/Trait tags so combat-relevant labels don't get mixed with matchmaking-only ones. Reserved value "Piercing" does NOT bypass damageReceivedModifiers (a target vulnerability keyed on "Piercing" still applies in full) — instead it bypasses the target's flat armor stat entirely (e.g. Sniper). Splash is NOT a damage type — it's already fully expressed by splashRadius below.

revealRange.description: Distance at which a stealthed unit becomes visible to a non-detector. Required if stealth is true.

detector.description: Reveals stealthed units within detectionRange (or full visionRange if detectionRange is omitted), ignoring their revealRange. Revealing means the stealthed unit becomes visible/targetable to ANY of the owning player's troops/buildings within that same local radius, not just to this detector itself — e.g. Radar Array has no attack of its own, so its detector flag only matters because it exposes the target to whatever else is nearby.

detectionRange.description: Optional override for detector's stealth-sight radius, when it should differ from this unit's own visionRange. Defaults to visionRange if omitted. Currently unused by any troop (Sniper's stealth detection uses its full visionRange) — added for symmetry with the building-side field of the same name (see data/buildings/schema.json), used by Tower.

revealsOnAttack.description: If true, attacking breaks this unit's own stealth until a few seconds pass without attacking.

empImmune.description: True if this troop is unaffected by the "emp" statusEffectOnHit type (Signal Ridge's EMP Turret — see game-design/05-troop-stat-schema.md's Status Effects section and data/buildings/emp_turret.json). Currently only Hot Air Balloon and Glider: both are unpowered/non-electronic (a hot-air envelope and an unpowered glider have no engine for an EMP to disable), unlike every other Air-domain unit.

cargoCapacity.description: How many SQUADS this troop can carry aboard it (not individual troop headcount) — a boarding squad occupies exactly one slot regardless of its own size. 0 = cannot transport. Capacity sums across every troop in the carrier's own squad (see game-design/07-data-architecture.md's SquadInstance.cargoSquadIds).

cargoAllowedTags.description: Domains/tags this unit is allowed to load as cargo, same mechanism as canTarget.

canLaunchCargoMidCombat.description: If true, cargo can be deployed mid-battle rather than only while idle/docked.

cargoRequiresBuildingDock.description: If true, this carrier can only board/unload its own cargo while sitting on a hex that carries a building capable of docking THIS carrier itself (i.e. a building whose own cargoAllowedTags, see data/buildings/schema.json, intersects this troop's Domain/tags — the same match CargoSystem.can_dock already uses). Unlike the Naval coastline rule (which is physics-driven: Land/Infantry cargo simply cannot stand on a water hex, so a Dock/Port/Shipyard/Harbour is implicitly required), Air has no terrain that blocks it from meeting cargo anywhere — so an Air-domain carrier needs this explicit opt-in flag to be building-gated at all. No dock/undock order is required and the building's own cargoCapacity is untouched by this — it's a pure landing-pad location check, not a slot reservation. Currently only Cargocopter sets this (must be on a Hangar hex to load/unload Infantry).

canBuildInfrastructure.description: If true, this unit can construct standalone Road/Bridge/Dock/Tower (see game-design/01-map-and-terrain.md and game-design/02-bases-and-buildings.md). Only Engineer has this today; the buildable set itself is fixed rather than per-unit, so a boolean is sufficient.

auras.description: Passive effects projected onto nearby units/buildings.

filter.description: Optional narrowing, e.g. a specific building type, or a Domain value (e.g. "Infantry", "Land") to restrict a troop-targeted aura to one domain. Matches exactly one value — a support unit that needs to cover multiple domains (e.g. Volt Truck covering Land/Air/Naval) carries one aura entry per domain rather than a list here.

effect.description: e.g. "damage_reduction", "slow", "production_boost", "suppress_targeting", "resource_siphon", "heal_over_time", "upkeep_reduction", "speed_boost", "attack_speed_boost". "resource_siphon" (target "enemy_buildings", filter "Resource") is binary like "suppress_targeting" — no magnitude — and fully redirects the building's production for the tick from its owner to the siphoning player; when multiple siphon sources reach the same building, only the closest wins (no stacking/summing).

magnitude.description: Always a plain number — never a unit-suffixed string like "6/s" or "+25%" — so the engine never has to parse it. Unit is implicit from effect: heal_over_time and upkeep_reduction are flat amounts (hp/s, and food+fuel upkeep each respectively — upkeep_reduction applies the same flat magnitude to both foodUpkeep and fuelUpkeep simultaneously, floored at 0 per troop), slow/production_boost/damage_reduction/speed_boost/attack_speed_boost are signed percent (e.g. -20 = -20%, 25 = +25%).

statusEffectOnHit.description: Temporary condition applied to the target on a successful hit, in addition to direct damage.

type.description: e.g. "freeze", "stun", "knockback", "emp". "stun" is a GLOBAL rule, not per-instance config: it always applies a fixed -30% move/attack-speed debuff once the lockout (duration) ends, lasting that same duration again — in addition to the full lockout itself. "knockback" is an instantaneous displacement (magnitude = hexes shoved back), not a timed lockout. "emp" is domain-conditional (Land: movement disabled but can still attack, for duration; Air: instant destroy, no duration; Infantry/Naval: no effect) and respects a target's empImmune flag. See game-design/05-troop-stat-schema.md's Status Effects section. Don't add fields here to configure the stun tail; author only duration/magnitude/chance for the lockout, the tail reuses duration and its -30%/-30% magnitude is fixed.

chance.description: Percent chance (0-100) the effect applies on a successful hit. Omitted/100 = always applies. E.g. a stun effect might set this to 30 for a 30% chance per hit.

foodUpkeep.description: Ongoing Food consumption while the unit exists.

fuelUpkeep.description: Ongoing Fuel consumption; rules vary by Domain (see game-design/03-resources.md).

productionTime.description: Training duration, counted down in the producing building's own queue.
